title: EntityClearer
body:
![Online Servers](https://img.shields.io/bstats/servers/10915?style=for-the-badge&label=online+servers)![Online Players](https://img.shields.io/bstats/players/10915?style=for-the-badge&label=online+players)

# EntityClearer

### This plugin requires Java 21 or higher and a Paper server.
The legacy Spigot version can be downloaded [here](https://www.spigotmc.org/resources/entityclearer-a-clearlagg-alternative.90802/update?update=598867).

#### It also works well with [FarmControl](https://modrinth.com/plugin/farmcontrol)!

---

![https://imgur.com/vRWV9K9.gif](https://imgur.com/vRWV9K9.gif)

![https://imgur.com/fGZHEcq.gif](https://imgur.com/fGZHEcq.gif)

![https://imgur.com/VaBn6Tl.gif](https://imgur.com/VaBn6Tl.gif)

## Features
* Custom interval
* Custom warning sounds
* Customizable entity list
* World whitelist
* Keep named entities
* Keep entities with a player riding them
* Remove entities with certain spawn reasons
* Remove only entities with x others nearby
* Action bar messages
* Chat messages
* Title/subtitle messages
* TPS monitoring
* MythicMobs support
* PlaceholderAPI placeholders
* And more!

---

You might ask, "**Why should I use this over something like ClearLagg?**"

EntityClearer not only adds action bar messages, but also warning sounds, world whitelists, and many other useful features!

Plugins like ClearLagg are often frowned upon because they remove *all* entities from the list in an attempt to "clear lag", but this can actually cause *more* lag since entities will just respawn shortly after.

EntityClearer takes a smarter approach: it only removes entities that are clustered together *(customizable in the config)*. This helps eliminate the worst lag offenders while also preventing mobs from immediately respawning. As a bonus, EntityClearer is still actively maintained. :)

---
